title: NAAMSE
description: Engineered the scalable core infrastructure for an evolutionary AI red-teaming framework and fuzzer, optimizing high-throughput execution pipelines for adversarial prompt injection. Architected data systems to support real-time attack mutation using genetic algorithms. Won 2nd place out of 1,500+ submissions at the Berkeley RDI AgentX competition; framework later forked by Mozilla's 0din team.

title: HASHIRU
description: Architected a budget-aware multi-agent orchestration framework (Hierarchical Agent System for Hybrid Intelligent Resource Utilization) enabling highly distributed workload management. Built robust cost-optimization controls to strictly manage resource compute constraints, inference expenses, and token budgeting across parallel LLM agent execution streams.

title: gem5 Vision
description: Engineered advanced search functionality and a comprehensive categorization system for the gem5 Resource Infrastructure, enhancing resource discovery for computer architecture researchers. Architected extensible database integrations to support diverse storage and retrieval options, providing developers with robust tools for managing simulator resources.

title: QuixFolio
description: An open-source, automated portfolio builder that allows users to deploy professional portfolios to GitHub Pages in minutes. Developed a template-driven engine that achieved over 1,000 page views in its first week of alpha launch.

title: Stadia Wireless
description: Reverse-engineered and bypassed Google Stadia Controller hardware constraints, implementing a websocket-based architecture to enable universal wireless connectivity. Accumulated 450+ stars on GitHub and featured in major publications including The Verge and Ars Technica.

title: VeggieBuddie
description: A diet-restriction assistant app that identifies safe-to-eat products by scanning ingredient images. Awarded 'Most Social Good Hack' at HackDavis and selected as a Top 20 global solution in the Google Solution Challenge.

title: Other Projects
description: A dynamically curated list of my other open-source contributions and technical experiments.
